title: Explain the ending of the TV series to me DC 21 24
display_title: Explain the ending of the TV series to me DC 21 24
wikitext:
[]: Explain the ending of the TV series to me (DC 21-24)             Quote Post by alih92 » Thu Jan 21, 2010 8:00 pm Did Shinji just dream all of it? Because If that is what happened then I'm perfectly happy with this ending.

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Thu Jan 21, 2010 8:17 pm Not a dream. It happened. I believe in episode 26 the text was something along the lines of (paraphrasing from memory here) "In the year 2016, instrumentality takes place. There's not enough time to show everything. This covers one case, Shinji Ikari."

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Jan 21, 2010 9:01 pm Episodes 25 and 26 is Instrumentality, such as seen in EoE. It just leaves out the cause of Instrumentality and goes straight to the characters within it.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Thu Jan 21, 2010 9:10 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Jan 21, 2010 9:56 pm Regardless of which episodes you would like explained, giving even a proper synopsis of them is in fact extremely difficult. There are layers of meaning in even the most obscure scenes and the final episodes are among the most dense in the series. The synopses of the episodes in the wiki may be of some assistance in aiding understanding. Beware of spoilers though.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Jan 22, 2010 12:38 am The Director's Cuts (or whatever you want to call them) of Episodes 21-24 don't really offer that much in explaining the last two episodes. They just more thoroughly venture into the situations already presented in the edition aired on television. SPOILER: Show
